perturbation.original_text: We also acknowledge and agree that no contract or agreement providing for the sale of the Company shall be deemed to exist between us and the Company unless and until a Sale Agreement has been executed and delivered by us and each of the other parties thereto, and we hereby waive, in advance, any claims (including, without limitation, breach of contract) in connection with the sale of the Company unless and until a Sale Agreement has been executed and delivered by us and each of the other parties thereto.
perturbation.changed_text: We also acknowledge and agree that no contract or agreement providing for the sale of the Company shall be deemed to exist between us and the Company unless and until a Sale Agreement has been executed and delivered by us and each of the other parties thereto, and we hereby waive, in advance, any claims (including, without limitation, breach of contract) in connection with the sale of the Company unless and until a Sale Agreement has been executed and delivered by us and each of the other parties thereto. However, preliminary letters of intent signed by both parties shall be considered binding agreements regarding exclusivity and confidentiality.
perturbation.explanation: By adding 'However, preliminary letters of intent signed by both parties shall be considered binding agreements regarding exclusivity and confidentiality', the clause now states that while a full sale agreement is needed for a sale to be binding, preliminary letters of intent are binding for specific aspects (exclusivity and confidentiality). This conflicts with the initial statement that *no contract or agreement* exists until a 'Sale Agreement' is fully executed. The first definition stated that 'no contract agreement providing for the sale of the Company', while the insertion makes the preliminary letters of intent for exclusivity and confidentiality to be 'binding agreements'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: We also agree that unless and until a Sale Agreement between the Company and us with respect to the acquisition of the Company has been executed and delivered by us and each of the other parties thereto, there shall not be any legal obligation of any kind whatsoever with respect to any such transaction by virtue of this agreement or any other written or oral expression with respect to such transaction except, in the case of this Agreement for the matters specifically agreed to herein.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction. The original text and the contradicted text both state that there is 'no legal obligation of any kind whatsoever' until a Sale Agreement is executed. However, the changed text adds that 'preliminary letters of intent signed by both parties shall be considered binding agreements regarding exclusivity and confidentiality'. This directly contradicts the original statement that no agreement exists until the final Sale Agreement. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction exists as the modified text creates a binding agreement before the Sale Agreement, which is not in line with the rest of the original contract.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties hereto regarding the subject matter hereof. This Agreement may be changed only by a written agreement signed by the parties hereto or their authorized representatives.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties hereto regarding the subject matter hereof. This Agreement may be changed only by a written agreement signed by the parties hereto or their authorized representatives, unless both parties verbally agree to a modification, which will then be documented in meeting minutes.
perturbation.explanation: The original agreement stipulates that changes can only be made through a signed written agreement. The change that states 'unless both parties verbally agree to a modification, which will then be documented in meeting minutes' allows for verbal modifications that are then documented, thus directly contradicting the initial requirement for a written agreement signed by both parties or their representatives. This creates an in-text contradiction by invalidating the written agreement being the only method to change the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Such remedies shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for a breach of this Agreement by us or our representatives but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or equity. Other This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties hereto regarding the subject matter hereof.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an exception to the original requirement that all modifications must be in writing and signed. By allowing verbal agreements to modify the contract (documented in meeting minutes), the changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text which states that 'This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties hereto regarding the subject matter hereof.' This implies that any changes not made through written agreement are not valid, directly conflicting with the altered text and creating an in-text contradiction according to the provided definition of Ambiguities.
